subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which of the following is considered a primary storage device in a computer?

step_by_step: Primary storage is volatile and directly accessible by the CPU (e.g., RAM).
Secondary storage is non-volatile and used for long-term data (e.g., HDD, Pen Drive).

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: What does RAID stand for in the context of computer storage?

step_by_step: RAID is a way of storing the same data in different places on multiple hard disks or solid-state drives to protect data in the case of a drive failure.

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which of these storage media has the highest data transfer speed?

step_by_step: SSDs use flash memory and have no moving parts, making them much faster than optical or magnetic disks.

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: A full backup of a system refers to:

options.text: Backing up all files on a selected volume or disk
Backing up data to the cloud only

solution.final_answer: Backing up all files on a selected volume or disk

step_by_step: Incremental backup only copies files changed since the last backup.

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which storage device is commonly used for sequential access of large amounts of data for long-term archiving?

step_by_step: Magnetic tapes are sequential access media, meaning data is read in order.
They are very cheap per GB and durable, making them ideal for massive backups.

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Cloud storage solutions like Google Drive or Dropbox primarily use which type of hardware for back-end storage?

options.text: Data centers with massive server farms

solution.final_answer: Data centers with massive server farms

step_by_step: Cloud providers store data in large, secure data centers across the globe.

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: The capacity of a standard single-layer DVD is approximately:

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which of the following is a non-volatile semiconductor storage medium?

step_by_step: Flash memory is used in SSDs and USB drives. It is electronic and retains data without power.

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: In storage technology, what is 'Hot Swapping'?

options.text: Replacing a computer component while it is still running
Cooling down a drive before replacement
Transferring data over a network at high speed
Switching between internal and external power

solution.final_answer: Replacing a computer component while it is still running

step_by_step: Hot swapping allows for adding or removing hardware (like a failed hard drive in a server) without shutting down the system.

subject: Computer Basics
question_text: Which unit is used to represent one quadrillion bytes (approx. 1024 Terabytes)?

step_by_step: KB -> MB -> GB -> TB -> PB -> EB -> ZB.
